title: Using unencrypted EBS volumes is security-sensitive
description: Using unencrypted EBS volumes is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotawscweAmazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) is a block-storage service for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2). EBS volumes can be encrypted, ensuring the security of both data-at-rest and data-in-transit between an instance and its attached EBS storage. In the case that adversaries gain physical access to the storage medium they are not able to access the data.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Ec2Volume:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: us-east-1a
      Size: 100
      Encrypted: false  # Sensitive - encryption disabled

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Ec2Volume:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: us-east-1a
      Size: 100  # Sensitive - encryption disabled by default
compliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  Ec2Volume:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: us-east-1a
      Size: 100
      Encrypted: true
